macro.intro: Inflationary pressures continue with CPI YoY at 330.29, up from 327.46. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield has slightly decreased to 1.92%, indicating a shift in investor sentiment. The Fed Funds Rate remains stable at 3.64%. No major economic events are scheduled today, allowing markets to focus on geopolitical developments.

snapshot.name: Gold (XAU/USD)
snapshot.note: Safe-haven demand amid Middle East tensions

snapshot.name: WTI Crude Oil
snapshot.note: Below $100 as China defies Hormuz blockade

setups.name: Gold Long Setup
setups.rationale: Gold is trading at $1985.50, benefiting from geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. With CPI YoY inflation at 330.29, gold remains a hedge against inflation. The lack of major economic events today supports a focus on geopolitical developments.

metrics.label: CPI YoY Inflation
metrics.text: High inflation supports gold as a hedge

metrics.label: Geopolitical Tensions
metrics.text: Middle East conflict boosts safe-haven demand

metrics.label: Economic event
metrics.text: No major events today, focus on geopolitical news

metrics.label: Fear & Greed Index
metrics.text: Not available, but geopolitical risks imply fear

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: De-escalation in Middle East could lower gold demand

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Unexpected Fed rate hike could strengthen USD

setups.hedge: Consider shorting USD/JPY if gold declines below $1960.

setups.name: S&P 500 Short Setup
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is at 4475.30, rallying on optimism over US-Iran talks. However, the IMF's growth downgrade for the Middle East and potential for renewed tensions could reverse gains.

metrics.label: Geopolitical Tensions
metrics.text: Potential for renewed conflict could impact markets

metrics.label: IMF Growth Forecast
metrics.text: Downgrade for Middle East could weigh on sentiment

metrics.label: Economic event
metrics.text: No major events today, focus on geopolitical news

metrics.label: Fear & Greed Index
metrics.text: Not available, but market optimism may be overdone

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Successful US-Iran talks could boost equities further

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Strong earnings reports could support index

setups.hedge: Consider buying VIX calls if S&P 500 rises above 4525.

setups.name: EUR/USD Long Setup
setups.rationale: EUR/USD is trading at 1.0950, with potential to test 1.1000 resistance. The stable Fed Funds Rate at 3.64% and improving Eurozone economic outlook support the euro.

metrics.label: Fed Funds Rate
metrics.text: Stable rate supports euro against USD

metrics.label: Economic event
metrics.text: No major events today, focus on macro trends

metrics.label: Fear & Greed Index
metrics.text: Not available, but euro strength suggests risk-on

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: US economic data surprise could strengthen USD

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Geopolitical tensions could increase USD demand

setups.hedge: Consider shorting GBP/USD if EUR/USD falls below 1.0880.

contrarian.body: WTI Crude Oil has dipped below $100, currently at $99.75, due to geopolitical tensions and China's defiance of the Hormuz blockade. With the IMF warning of adverse scenarios, oil could rebound if tensions escalate.
contrarian.warning: A resolution in the Middle East could lead to a further decline in oil prices. Monitor geopolitical developments closely.

avoid.reason: Flight cancellations amid Middle East conflict could impact revenues.
Record diesel spending due to Middle East tensions increases operational costs.
Potential for increased volatility due to geopolitical tensions.
IMF growth downgrade could impact sector outlook.

IMF warns of adverse scenarios affecting growth outlook.
